_comment: Key illustrated events for Chapter 6 (developmental chapter; verified shape of Shivaji's dual education, framed representatively). 'anchor' is an EXACT substring of chapters/ch06.md. 'refs' are entity slugs from reference/index.json. 'image' filled by tools/refgen.py AFTER references are signed off.

events.title: Not a sword — an account book
events.scene_prompt: Indoors at the Lal Mahal: the grey, exacting old administrator Dadoji Konddev sits with an older boy Shivaji (about eleven) over an open account book and land-maps spread on a low desk, a reed pen and ledgers around them. Dadoji points at a row of figures, teaching; the boy listens, a little impatient but attentive. Warm lamplit study scene — learning to govern, not just fight.

events.title: Lean, quick, and hard to knock down
events.anchor: he learned to wrestle in the red earth
events.scene_prompt: Outdoor training ground in Pune: the older boy Shivaji (about eleven) trains hard in the martial arts among tough, hill-bred Mavala fighters — wrestling in red earth, practising with sword and spear, a horse nearby. Energetic, dusty, vigorous; his instructors lean and weathered, not going easy on him. Sunlit, spirited.

events.title: Fair, rank or no rank
events.anchor: watched him sit and listen to farmers and weighers and complainers
events.scene_prompt: A village justice scene: Dadoji Konddev sits hearing a dispute among Maval villagers — farmers and a grain-weigher and a complainer — patiently working out who is owed what and making it come out fair, holding even a well-dressed headman to account. The older boy Shivaji stands at his side, watching closely and learning. Maval village setting, ordinary people, a quiet lesson in fairness.

events.title: Learning the hills and their people
events.anchor: Shivaji rode out into the country his family was now responsible for
events.scene_prompt: The older boy Shivaji (about eleven) rides on horseback through the folded Maval hill-country, pausing among ordinary Maval villagers — farmers and herders — who greet him warmly as he asks after their harvest and their wells. He is curious and listening, not lordly; they are poor, proud hill people. Sweeping green-brown hills, terraced fields, a village in its fold. Warm, expansive — the bond between the boy and the hill people beginning.
